tasks.description: Add transcript POST endpoint and gateway broadcast method for LiveKit → WS bridge.
steps: Add `broadcastTranscript(sessionId, speaker, text, timestamp)` method to `ArenaGateway` — broadcasts `{ type: 'transcript', speaker, text, timestamp }` to all WS clients in the session
Create DTO `backend/src/arena/dto/post-transcript.dto.ts` — fields: `speaker` (string, required), `text` (string, required), `timestamp` (number, optional, defaults to Date.now())
Add `POST /arena/sessions/:id/transcript` endpoint to `ArenaController` — validates session exists, calls `arenaGateway.broadcastTranscript()`, returns 204
Mark the endpoint `@AllowAnonymous()` since the agent worker calls it without user auth (same pattern as existing arena endpoints)

tasks.description: Add LiveKit data channel sending to gateway for WS → LiveKit bridge.
steps: Inject `LivekitService` into `ArenaGateway` (add to constructor)
Add `sendDataToRoom(roomName, payload)` method to `LivekitService` — uses `RoomServiceClient.sendData()` to send a JSON-encoded buffer to the room with `topic: 'external-agent-msg'`

In `ArenaGateway.handleClientMessage()`, when processing a `message` type: after the existing `broadcastToSession()` call, also look up the session's `roomName` and if active, call `livekitService.sendDataToRoom(roomName, { type: 'external_agent_message', speaker, text })`. Wrap in try/catch so WS flow is not broken if LiveKit send fails.
Update `ArenaModule` providers/imports if needed for the new dependency

tasks.description: Extend room metadata with sessionId and backendUrl so the agent worker can post transcripts.
steps: In `LivekitService.generateArenaToken()`, add `sessionId: session.id` to the metadata JSON object
Add `backendUrl` to metadata: read from `BACKEND_URL` env var (default `http://localhost:4200`)
Update `ArenaMetadata` interface in `agent/src/arena-agent.ts` to include optional `sessionId?: string` and `backendUrl?: string` fields
Add `BACKEND_URL` to `backend/.env.example` and `agent/.env.example`

tasks.description: Agent worker posts transcripts to backend after each assistant turn (LiveKit → WS direction).
steps: In `agent/src/main.ts`, after the arena session starts, subscribe to `ConversationItemAdded` events for assistant messages

When an assistant message is finalized, extract the speaker name from `[Name]: text` format and POST to `${backendUrl}/arena/sessions/${sessionId}/transcript` with `{ speaker, text, timestamp: Date.now() }`
Use native `fetch()` for the HTTP call (Node 18+ built-in, no new dependencies)
Wrap in try/catch with a warning log on failure — never let a transcript post failure crash the agent or disrupt voice flow
Skip posting if `sessionId` or `backendUrl` are missing from metadata (backwards compatible with non-bridge sessions)

tasks.description: Agent worker listens for LiveKit data channel messages and injects external agent input (WS → LiveKit direction).
steps: In `agent/src/main.ts`, after the arena session starts, listen for `RoomEvent.DataReceived` on `ctx.room`
Parse incoming data messages: expect JSON `{ type: 'external_agent_message', speaker: string, text: string }`
Filter by topic `'external-agent-msg'` to avoid processing unrelated data messages
When a valid external agent message arrives, inject it into the conversation via `session.generateReply({ userInput: '[External agent ${speaker} says]: ${text}' })`
Add a short debounce/cooldown (e.g. 1 second) to avoid flooding the LLM if multiple external agents send messages simultaneously
Log incoming external messages at debug level

tasks.description: ArenaSessionContext accumulates WS transcript and agent_message events into state.
steps: In `ArenaSessionContext`, add `wsMessages` state: `useState<TranscriptMessage[]>([])`

In the WS `onmessage` handler, add cases for `type: 'agent_message'` and `type: 'transcript'` — convert to `TranscriptMessage` format: `{ id: 'ws-${timestamp}-${speaker}', sender: 'agent', text: '[${speaker}]: ${text}', isFinal: true, timestamp, source: 'ws' }`
Expose `wsMessages` from the context value
Update `ArenaSessionContextValue` interface to include `wsMessages: TranscriptMessage[]`
Clear `wsMessages` in `endSession()`

tasks.description: Extend TranscriptMessage type with optional source field for origin tracking.
steps: Add optional `source?: 'livekit' | 'ws'` field to `TranscriptMessage` interface in `ui-web/src/lib/types.ts`
In `use-conversation-transcript.ts`, tag messages with `source: 'livekit'` when creating them
This field enables deduplication and lets the UI distinguish message origins if needed in the future

tasks.description: ArenaRoom merges LiveKit and WS transcript streams with deduplication.
steps: In `ArenaRoom`, import `useArenaSession` and get `wsMessages` from context
Create a `useMergedTranscript` hook or inline `useMemo` that merges `messages` (from `useConversationTranscript`) with `wsMessages` (from context)
Sort merged array by timestamp

Deduplicate: if a WS `transcript` message has the same speaker and similar text (e.g. starts-with match) as a LiveKit message within a 5-second window, drop the WS duplicate. This handles the case where the host UI sees the same voice agent message from both LiveKit transcription and the backend's WS broadcast.
Pass the merged messages to `ArenaTranscript` and `useActiveSpeaker`
External agent messages (from WS `agent_message` type) will appear in the transcript with their speaker name and color, using the same `[Name]: text` parsing already in `ArenaTranscript`
